name: Citsukha
dates_notes: Dated by guru-paramparā and citation by datable later authors; commonly placed c. 1220 CE.
core_thesis: Citsukha brings Vivaraṇa-Advaita to its dialectical maturity. Ānandabodha's inferential apparatus (*Nyāya-Makaranda*; *Pramāṇa-Mālā*) is refashioned into a comprehensive *khaṇḍana* of every realist alternative on offer: pre-Gaṅgeśa Nyāya, Mīmāṃsā, Bhedābheda, and the proto-Dvaita and Viśiṣṭādvaita positions then crystallising. Four technical doctrines anchor the work. First, *cit* / *ātman* is *svaprakāśa*, self-luminous: defined as that which is *aparokṣa-vyavahāra-yogya* (fit for direct cognitive employment) without depending on a further cognition ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 1](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/1), *svaprakāśatva-paricheda*). Second, the world is *anirvacanīya-mithyā*, established by formal *anumāna* ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 2](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/2), *dṛśyatvānumāna* and parallel *anumānas*); *mithyā* is throughout *sad-asad-vilakṣaṇa* (different from both real and unreal) and never collapses into *asat* ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 2](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/2), *mithyātva-lakṣaṇa-paricheda*). Third, avidyā is *bhāva-rūpā* with Brahman as both locus and object ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 3](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/3), *avidyā-paricheda*). Fourth, the Nyāya category-scheme is destroyed: *sāmānya*, *viśeṣa*, *samavāya*, and *bheda* itself are shown to be analytically indeterminable ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 4](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/4), *padārtha-khaṇḍana*). The destruction proceeds by dilemma — for each category, a binary characterisation is exhausted and shown self-defeating, leaving the category itself unintelligible ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 4](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/4), *bheda-khaṇḍana*). Citsukha works throughout in the technical idiom of the pre-Gaṅgeśa Naiyāyikas, beating them on their own turf. The *Tattva-Pradīpikā* becomes the standard Advaita reference for the dialectic of *mithyātva* and is presupposed by the entire *Advaita-Siddhi* / *Nyāyāmṛta* exchange (cf. [*Advaita-Siddhi* 1](cite://madhusudana/advaita-siddhi/1), opening *paricheda*).

engaged_works.title: Tattva-Pradipika
engaged_works.summary: Vast polemical *prakaraṇa* — universally cited as the *Citsukhī* — organised in four *paricchedas* and devoted to systematic defence of the Vivaraṇa-Advaita theses against every available realist opponent. The first *paricheda* defines and defends *svaprakāśatva*: *cit* is self-luminous, characterised as *avedyatve sati aparokṣa-vyavahāra-yogyatva* (fit for direct cognitive employment without itself being an object) ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 1.1–7](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/1.1-7)). The second gives the canonical formal *anumānas* for *anirvacanīya-mithyātva*, most famously the *dṛśyatvānumāna* ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 2](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/2), *mithyātva-anumāna-paricheda*), while insisting throughout that *mithyā* is *sad-asad-vilakṣaṇa* and never *asat* ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 2](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/2), *mithyātva-lakṣaṇa-paricheda*). The third defends *bhāva-rūpā-avidyā* with Brahman as both locus and object ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 3.1–14](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/3.1-14)). The fourth deconstructs the Nyāya categories ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 4](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/4)). Each category — *sāmānya*, *viśeṣa*, *samavāya*, *bheda* — is met by a destructive dilemma: a closed disjunction of possible analyses is exhausted, each horn shown to be self-defeating or vacuous ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 4](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/4), *padārtha-khaṇḍana*). *Bheda* in particular is examined as itself a candidate substance, attribute, or relation; each option fails ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 4](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/4), *bheda-khaṇḍana*). Bhedābheda and the early Madhva-line account of substantial difference are refuted in the same idiom ([*Tattva-Pradīpikā* 4](cite://citsukha/tattva-pradipika/4), closing portion). Pratyagrūpa's *Nayanaprasādinī* is the standard sub-commentary. The *Citsukhī* becomes the technical encyclopaedia presupposed by Madhusūdana's *Advaita-Siddhi* (1, opening *paricheda*), by Vyāsatīrtha's *Nyāyāmṛta*, and by every later participant in the *mithyātva* debate.

quoted_by.what_quoted: Madhusūdana presupposes Citsukha's *Tattva-Pradīpikā* throughout the *Advaita-Siddhi*; Citsukha's formal *anumāna*-armature for *anirvacanīya-mithyātva*, his *svaprakāśa*-definition, and his *bheda-khaṇḍana* are the technical apparatus the *Advaita-Siddhi* both inherits and defends.
Vyāsatīrtha's *Nyāyāmṛta* takes Citsukha's formal *mithyātva-anumāna* as the principal Advaitin position to refute.

quoted_by.locus: engagement with anti-Citsukha arguments in the polemical sections
quoted_by.what_quoted: Madhva's *Anuvyākhyāna* engages the structurally same Advaita position that Citsukha later systematizes.
